babyDevelopment: The baby is practicing 'breathing' using amniotic fluid. Toenails and fingernails are fully formed. The baby's skin is becoming smoother as fat fills out. The baby is likely spending most of its time in a head-down position (cephalic).
bodyChanges: You may experience Braxton Hicks contractions more frequently as your body prepares for labor. Your center of gravity has shifted significantly. Some women notice 'numbness' or 'tingling' in their hands and fingers.
tips: Prepare your hospital bag if you haven't yet
Incorporate more fiber and stay hydrated to manage constipation

dont: Panic if you feel mild contractions (check with doc if regular)

travelAdvisory.note: Stay close to home as you approach the final weeks.

milestoneDescription: Most babies have moved into a head-down position in preparation for birth.
